title: alansmia stella
intro: Alansmia stella, also known as the "Queen of the Andes," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America, particularly in the Andean region. Alansmia stella is a type of polypod fern, characterized by its unique, long-lasting flowers and medicinal properties. The plant contains flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. Alansmia stella is traditionally used in herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, coughs, and asthma.
